skills.name: reconcile-current-state
skills.human_summary: Proves current branch, runtime, and handoff state before acting.
skills.llm_trigger: Use when continuing prior Claude Code, Codex, Antigravity, or subagent work; when handoff notes may be stale; when a previous agent claimed progress; or when current repository, runtime, CI, PR, or endpoint state must be rechecked before acting.

skills.name: audit-agent-history
skills.human_summary: Inventories local agent histories without dumping raw transcripts.
skills.llm_trigger: Use when analyzing local Codex, Claude Code, or Antigravity history stores; when comparing current provider storage; when improving retention; when recovering prior context; when deriving reusable skills from past agent work; or when AI usage assessment needs a redacted Provider Evidence Packet before scoring.

skills.name: guard-side-effects
skills.human_summary: Classifies side effects and approval needs before risky actions.
skills.llm_trigger: Use when a requested or planned action may mutate files, git state, remotes, production, money, access, secrets, user data, provider homes, or external systems.

skills.name: close-live-work
skills.human_summary: Closes work with runtime, endpoint, release, or user-flow evidence.
skills.llm_trigger: Use when local tests or CI passed but runtime, endpoint, release, worker, queue, browser, mobile, or user-facing evidence still matters before claiming completion.

skills.name: align-agent-home
skills.human_summary: Separates shareable provider config from local runtime state.
skills.llm_trigger: Use when agent home config, guidance, skills, rules, hooks, manifests, tool profiles, private sync boundaries, or provider-specific setup need alignment across Codex, Claude Code, or Antigravity.

skills.name: recover-worktree-branch
skills.human_summary: Proves branch and worktree state before recovery or cleanup.
skills.llm_trigger: Use when a worktree path vanished, a branch is detached or missing, checkout state is confusing, cleanup is requested, or branch recovery needs proof before deletion or recreation.

skills.name: agent-ecosystem-radar
skills.graduation_rationale: The one-pass radar is useful, but it duplicates the four-stage research, evaluation, comparison, and recommendation chain. Keep it until a routed cookbook path proves the same outcome without a dedicated orchestrator skill.
skills.human_summary: Runs the research, evaluation, comparison, and recommendation skill set.
skills.llm_trigger: Use when the user wants research, comparison, and recommendation in one pass for external agent tools, skills, plugins, MCP servers, hooks, or workflow frameworks.

skills.name: research-agent-ecosystem
skills.graduation_rationale: The research boundary is clear and source-backed, but it still needs representative workflow examples and activation proof before it becomes part of the stable core.
skills.human_summary: Collects source-backed external agent workflow candidates.
skills.llm_trigger: Use when only current source gathering is needed for agent tools, skills, plugins, MCP servers, hooks, provider features, or workflow frameworks before comparison or upgrade planning.

skills.name: compare-agent-workflows
skills.graduation_rationale: The comparison contract is useful after research exists, but the package needs more end-to-end examples that show candidate scoring and rejected items before promotion.
skills.human_summary: Scores researched workflow candidates against GroundLine scope.
skills.llm_trigger: Use when comparing two or more researched agent tools, skills, plugins, MCP servers, hooks, workflow frameworks, or provider features against GroundLine scope and supported runtimes.

skills.name: recommend-groundline-upgrades
skills.graduation_rationale: Upgrade recommendation is the right decision surface, but it needs more proof that agents park watch and reject items instead of expanding the release scope.
skills.human_summary: Turns research and comparison into adopt, adapt, watch, or reject decisions.
skills.llm_trigger: Use when research or comparison findings already exist and need to become GroundLine upgrade candidates, adoption decisions, source registry entries, skills, references, scripts, docs, hooks, or MCP recommendations.

skills.name: evaluate-agent-capability
skills.graduation_rationale: Single-candidate evaluation has a strong output contract, but it still needs a sanitized provider activation row and a short worked example before promotion.
skills.human_summary: Scores one existing tool, skill, plugin, MCP server, hook, agent, or workflow pack before adoption.
skills.llm_trigger: Use when evaluating one existing agent tool, skill, plugin, MCP server, hook, agent, prompt pack, or workflow framework for quality, security, context cost, maintenance, and GroundLine fit.

skills.name: evaluate-ai-usage-maturity
skills.graduation_rationale: The maturity assessment is valuable and well-scoped, but promotion requires a redacted Provider Evidence Packet example and live activation proof without raw transcript storage.
skills.human_summary: Assesses AI workflow maturity from artifacts or a redacted Provider Evidence Packet.
skills.llm_trigger: Use when assessing a person or team's AI usage maturity, AI fluency, agent workflow habits, verification discipline, tool orchestration, reuse, automation, safety, and next improvement steps from artifacts or a redacted Provider Evidence Packet.

skills.name: package-agent-task
skills.graduation_rationale: The task packet contract is exercised by staged dogfood and live handoff proof, and it is central to resumed high-context work. Promote after the v0.3.3 install doctor passes from a fresh provider install.
skills.human_summary: Turns broad or resumed requests into concise LLM-ready task packets.
skills.llm_trigger: Use when a broad, ambiguous, multi-agent, resumed, or high-context request needs an LLM-ready task packet with goal, context, constraints, non-goals, mutation boundary, success criteria, verification, and handoff.

skills.name: hold-the-line
skills.graduation_rationale: Scope control matches repeated user behavior and has a clear contract, but it needs cookbook examples and provider rows before becoming stable.
skills.human_summary: Stops scope growth and chooses finish, budget, defer, watch, or reject before more work starts.
skills.llm_trigger: Use when a request, task, research thread, plugin package, or active change set starts expanding with new ideas, extra tools, broad cleanup, or unclear finish criteria before the current work is closed.

skills.name: polish-release-candidate
skills.graduation_rationale: Release polish is useful after scope lock, but it overlaps adjacent release skills and needs clearer examples before promotion.
skills.human_summary: Runs final docs, duplicate, privacy, gate, and commit-plan cleanup before release judgment.
skills.llm_trigger: Use when scope is locked and a release candidate needs final cleanup, docs polish, duplicate or stale surface review, privacy checks, validation gate ordering, commit split planning, or public readiness review before a ship decision.

skills.name: stabilize-release-cut
skills.graduation_rationale: The release cut skill has a canonical contract, current release dogfood, and sanitized provider proof for release-cut routing. Promote after package sync and post-install verification remain green.
skills.human_summary: Locks release scope, classifies remaining work, and proves release readiness.
skills.llm_trigger: Use when a growing change set, plugin package, skill set, or release candidate needs final scope lock, change budget, must-fix triage, dogfood evidence, release gates, regression checks, or a ship decision.

skills.name: compare-release-delta
skills.graduation_rationale: Post-release delta review is important, but it depends on a published patch and install evidence. Keep it out of the active core until v0.3.3 is released and compared against the prior version.
skills.human_summary: Compares deployed and previous versions with runtime, install, regression, and rollback evidence.
skills.llm_trigger: Use when a deployed release must be compared with a previous version, expected changes, runtime evidence, regression signals, install state, rollback notes, or post-deploy checklist items.

skills.name: evaluate-groundline-pack
skills.human_summary: Reviews repository readiness, skill completeness, safety, and release fitness.
skills.llm_trigger: Use when reviewing GroundLine itself for repository readiness, skill completeness, trigger clarity, documentation coverage, safety posture, tests, release quality, or public package fitness.

skills.name: curate-groundline-skills
skills.graduation_rationale: Portfolio curation is now used for this release line, but it needs stable graduation examples and schema expectations before becoming a supported maintainer skill.
skills.human_summary: Maintains the skill portfolio taxonomy and create, adapt, merge, split, deprecate, or reject decisions.
skills.llm_trigger: Use when deciding whether GroundLine should create, adapt, merge, split, deprecate, or reject skills, references, scripts, agents, hooks, MCP recommendations, or docs.
